reply: $16.7K/month from one AI-automated channel with zero editors. the economics of content creation are broken in the best way.
practical enterprise AI adoption sequence is what most companies need, not another model benchmark. the gap isn't capability — it's knowing where to start.
recursive agent flows — using AI to define goals for other AI — is the unlock. execution quality determines whether this compounds value or errors
ServiceNow has workflow depth, CRM vendors have user surface. in enterprise AI, the winner combines both — deep automation with the interface people already use.
121 compromised packages across 84 names is a reminder that the npm supply chain is fundamentally broken.
workflow automation still feels like connecting APIs and hoping nothing breaks.
faster workflows come from how tools fit around the work, not better tools alone.
AI eliminating the blank page makes starting frictionless, but it also blurs the line between work and overwork.
consumer-first for AI interfaces is the right bet.
one person running 6 YouTube channels with AI is the future of media.
